content.problemStatement:
Write a program to separate odd and even integers of the given array and print them on two different lines in the same original order.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single positive integer NNN - the length of array.
The second line of input contains NNN space separated integers - the elements of the array.


Output Format

Output XXX space separated integers on the first line - the odd elements of the array, if their total count is XXX. The order must be same as in given array.
Output YYY space separated integers on the second line - the even elements of the array, if their total count is YYY. Again, the order must be the same.
constraints: All array elements are positive integers <= 100000
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 100000) – the length of the array. The second line contains N positive integers (each ≤ 100000) separated by single spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output two lines. The first line lists all odd numbers of the given array in their original order, separated by a single space. If there is no odd number, output an empty line. The second line lists all even numbers in their original order, separated by a single space. If there is no even number, output an empty line. The outputs of consecutive test cases follow one another without extra blank lines.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: odds are 3,9,1,7; evens are 4,2. Second test case: odds 1,3,5; evens 2,4. Third test case has no odds, so the first line is empty and all numbers are even.
All numbers are odd, so the first line prints them and the second line is empty.
Only one even number, so the odd line is empty and the even line prints 2.

solution.approach: We scan the array once while keeping two lists – one for odd numbers and one for even numbers. Because we only need to preserve the original order, we simply append each element to the appropriate list as we read it. After processing a test case we print the odd list followed by a newline and then the even list. This is O(N) time per test case and uses O(N) extra space for the two output lists (which can be avoided by printing on the fly, but the list approach keeps the code simple and clear).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) for the whole input
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) per test case for storing odds and evens (output size)

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());for(int t=0;t<T;t++){int n=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());List<String> odds=new ArrayList<>();List<String> evens=new ArrayList<>();for(int i=0;i<n;i++){int x=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());if((x&1)==1) odds.add(String.valueOf(x));else evens.add(String.valueOf(x));}sb.append(String.join(" ",odds)).append('\n');sb.append(String.join(" ",evens));if(t!=T-1) sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
